body.title: CLOSED: Palace node edit forms with ADC_EDIT_TOKEN auth
body.result: GET /palace-edit/{path} + POST with token auth; edit link on node pages; 5 new tests — commit 5d98652
body.description:
Extend the read-only palace browser (T-web-palace-read) with edit capability. Palace nodes contain goals, decisions, hypotheses — write access must be gated.

Auth: bearer token via ADC_EDIT_TOKEN env var (if unset, edit routes return 403). Token passed as a query param or Authorization header. Simple but sufficient for a single-user homelab.

Edit routes:
  GET  /palace/{path}/edit  — edit form pre-filled with current content
  POST /palace/{path}/edit  — save updated content (auth required)
  POST /goals/{id}/status   — update goal status (auth required)

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; write via IGOR_HOME_DB_URL to adc.palace; auth via ADC_EDIT_TOKEN env var
**Scope boundary:** edit existing nodes only. No node creation via web (that's /decided + /goal skills). Auth is bearer token — not full session auth.
**Test plan:** POST /palace/{path}/edit without token → 403. POST with valid token → 200 and node updated in DB. Edit form pre-fills with current content.
